name: 英文模拟考试 - 入门级
subject: english

questions.stem: Which word is the opposite of 'hot'?
questions.analysis: The opposite of 'hot' is 'cold'. While 'cool' and 'freezing' are related to temperature, they don't directly mean the opposite.
The sentence talks about a past event (yesterday), so we need the past tense. 'Went' is the past tense of 'go'.
In English, with the third person singular (she), we use 'doesn't' for negation. The correct structure is 'she doesn't like'.

questions.stem: What time ___ you usually wake up?
questions.analysis: With 'you', we use 'do' in the simple present tense for questions. The answer is 'do'.
Before consonant sounds, we use 'a'. 'Cat' starts with a consonant sound, so 'a cat'. 'Dog' starts with a consonant sound, so 'a dog'. Answer: a/a.
The correct spelling is 'necessary' - with one 'c' and two 's's. A useful mnemonic: one collar and two sleeves.

questions.stem: Complete the sentence: If I ___ a lot of money, I would travel around the world.
questions.analysis: This is a conditional sentence (If clause). For hypothetical situations in present, we use 'had' (past tense) in the if-clause.

questions.stem: The teacher said that she ___ us tomorrow.
questions.analysis: In reported speech, 'will teach' becomes 'would teach' when the main clause is in past tense.

questions.stem: After finishing breakfast, Tom ___ to school by bus.
questions.analysis: This describes a habitual action (routine), so we use the simple present tense 'goes'.

questions.stem: Read the passage and answer: 'My name is John. I am a teacher. I work at a school in the city. I have 30 students in my class. I like my job very much.' - How many students does John have?
questions.analysis: According to the passage, John clearly states 'I have 30 students in my class.' The answer is 30 students.
